title: Linked Worktrees and Parallel Branch Operations · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer linked worktrees and parallel branch operations into a new multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish common repository data from per-worktree HEAD, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.
Add branch, detached, orphan, and relative-path worktrees; then lock, unlock, repair, and move them with path and device constraints.
Reason about checked-out branch exclusivity, shared refs, concurrent fetch or maintenance, and safe cross-worktree branch deletion and rewrite.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G54 is a lab in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish common repository data from per-worktree head, index, refs, operation state, config, and administrative links.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-parallel-operation-policy-1uezfg" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Parallel Operation Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-parallel-operation-policy-1uezfg
# scenario-key: trace-1uezfg probe-gfzeu1 boundary-ezfg1u owner-1uezfgx fault-1uezfgz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1u candidate=b00b1fg
# hypothesis: a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved
git worktree list --porcelain
# expected: porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state
# observation: the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason
# changed-case: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision
# after-ref: main=a11ce1u candidate=c0ffeeg
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair
# transfer-limit: worktrees share refs and objects, so concurrent real maintenance and rewrites need coordination
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair. Competency probe: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Parallel Operation Policy through the concrete multi-worktree ownership and cleanup map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.

hints: Name the Gita Parallel Operation Policy precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G63 is a lab in a disposable repository. a stale administrative entry is locking the release branch after its directory moved. The learner must predict porcelain output separates paths, HEAD IDs, branches, lock reasons, and prunable state, run git worktree list --porcelain, explain the release worktree points to a missing path and is locked with a documented owner reason, test this changed condition: move the main repository path and require worktree repair before any prune decision, and preserve this recovery boundary: lock valuable external worktrees and back up untracked outputs before administrative repair. Competency probe: Design named purpose, directory, branch, owner, lock, tool-cache, secret, cleanup, and handoff rules for parallel work.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Worktrees isolate hooks, config, ignored build outputs, and secrets by default.
